requirement: Final benchmark should contain 20-50 accepted tasks after QA and filtering.
source_note: Playbook project target and user benchmark-size requirement.

requirement: Accepted tasks should preserve a dev/test split.
source_note: Playbook split and difficulty design requirement.

requirement: Task families should be mixed and not mostly direct theorem proving.
Accepted tasks should span increasing human-time horizons, not only T0/T1/T2 calibration.
source_note: Playbook time-horizon design and user long-horizon objective.

requirement: Each release task should include public prompt and public Lean scaffold files.
Each release task should have hidden reference proof and hidden semantic PinCheck.
source_note: Playbook Lean grader architecture and hidden semantic pins sections.

requirement: Accepted tasks should include plausible wrong submissions that fail for meaningful reasons.
source_note: User QA requirement and playbook local validation loop.

requirement: Scoring should use Lean wherever possible and provide validation commands.
Submissions should be scanned for forbidden constructs before Lean grading.
Axiom usage should be audited or governed by a documented policy.
Task metadata should include family, domain, human-time, skills, scaffold sensitivity, and failure modes.
Task metadata, run results, and failure labels should have schemas.
run_results should represent successes_out_of_k and pass@k consistently.
The repo should support one-shot, lookup, and lookup plus iterative compile/debug scaffold variants.
A prospective evaluation protocol should define the primary accepted-task scaffold sweep before broad model runs.
source_note: Report-quality requirement derived from playbook calibration outputs.

requirement: Committed provider rows should be analyzed separately from local QA and against the planned primary sweep.
source_note: Report-quality requirement for not overstating smoke rows.

requirement: The report should compare real model performance across scaffolds, ideally pass@10.
source_note: Playbook scaffold interpretation and calibration outputs.

requirement: Run rows should link transcripts and carry failure labels for review.
Frontier/open-model runs should provide evidence beyond local QA.
Public export should include public assets and exclude hidden references and wrong submissions.
The METR-style report and plots should regenerate from committed CSVs.
source_note: Playbook report shape and user reporting requirement.

requirement: Difficulty audit should include proof length, tactic profile, automation dominance, hidden-pin strength, and model-solvability estimates.
Accepted tasks should have hard reviewer-style notes and benchmark-grade caveats.
Reviewer-facing task quality matrix should join metadata, difficulty signals, caveats, and next-review actions.
source_note: Research-report quality requirement for task-level auditability.

requirement: Hidden-pin audit should distinguish public-stage wrong failures from wrong submissions that reach semantic pins.
source_note: Research-report quality requirement for semantic-check evidence.

requirement: Run-result integrity audit should verify transcripts, score vectors, failure labels, and pass@k arithmetic.
source_note: Research-report quality requirement for run-data validity.

requirement: Claim-evidence audit should map artifact, report, performance, and benchmark-status claims to evidence strength and limits.
source_note: Research-report quality requirement for avoiding overclaims.

requirement: Release decision log should translate evidence audits into explicit pass/caution/block gates and next actions.
source_note: Research-report quality requirement for reviewable go/no-go claims.

requirement: Human-time estimates should be separately reviewed or measured, not inferred from model pass rates.
Hosted Taiga/Env Linter QA should be run before delivery/freeze.
A clean regeneration trail should record toolchain, commands, counts, and artifact hashes.
Candidate tasks should be separated from accepted tasks and pruned aggressively.
source_note: Playbook candidate generation and filtering guidance.

requirement: Formalization tasks should use semantic pins rather than brittle exact-text matching.
